slides.title: Exploring Space
bullets: Prepare to read, discuss and write
slides.narration: Welcome to Grade 5 English, Unit 2: Exploring Space. Welcome to Unit 2! In this unit, you will explore the exciting world of space and discovery. You will read about how people study the stars, build amazing projects, and work together to achieve great things. Along the way, you will learn new vocabulary, practise important grammar skills, and develop your writing and speaking abilities. In this short lecture, preview the key ideas and prepare for the independent lesson.

slides.title: What you will achieve
bullets: Use and understand 25 new vocabulary words related to systems, science, and social values
Identify and use indefinite pronouns such as everyone, somebody, nothing, and anything
Read and understand an informative passage about space exploration
slides.narration: By the end of this unit, you will be able to use and understand 25 new vocabulary words related to systems, science, and social values. you will also identify and use indefinite pronouns such as everyone, somebody, nothing, and anything. you will also read and understand an informative passage about space exploration. Keep these goals in mind as you work.

slides.title: Language for precise thinking
slides.narration: Your vocabulary work begins with these words: independence, issue, empire, expose, glare, source. Listen to each pronunciation in the vocabulary lab, study its meaning in context, and use the word accurately in your own discussion and writing.

slides.title: Read critically
slides.narration: The reading sequence includes Exploring Space: source text 1 and Exploring Space: source text 2. Read actively. Identify each text's central ideas, notice viewpoint and reliability, and support every inference with relevant evidence from the text.

slides.title: What Are Indefinite Pronouns?
bullets: Indefinite pronouns are special words that we use when we want to talk about people or things in a general way, without being specific. They are called...
slides.narration: The first language focus is What Are Indefinite Pronouns?. Indefinite pronouns are special words that we use when we want to talk about people or things in a general way, without being specific. They are called 'indefinite' because they do not point to a definite or particular person or thing. For example, instead of saying: Notice the structure in the model examples, then apply it deliberately in connected sentences and paragraphs.

slides.title: Use English for a real purpose
bullets: Explain, support and improve your ideas
slides.narration: In speaking, you will complete Speaking 1: Astronaut Interview Role-Play. Organise your ideas, use evidence and respond clearly to questions. In writing, you will plan, draft, check and revise. Use the success criteria before submitting your work.

slides.title: You are ready to begin
slides.narration: You are ready to begin. Start with the vocabulary lab, continue through reading and comprehension, apply the grammar focus, and complete the speaking and writing practices. Use AI English for hints and feedback, and bring your questions to the live sessions.
